children.heading: .—Establishment of Unified Carrier Registration System
children.label: a .—Establishment of Unified Carrier Registration System

children.text: the current Department of Transportation identification number system, the single State registration system under section 14504;1See References in Text note below.1
the registration system contained in this chapter and the financial responsibility information system under section 13906; and
the service of process agent systems under sections 503 and 13304.

children.heading: .—Role as Clearinghouse and Depository of Information
children.label: b .—Role as Clearinghouse and Depository of Information
children.text: The Unified Carrier Registration System shall serve as a clearinghouse and depository of information on, and identification of, all foreign and domestic motor carriers, motor private carriers, brokers, freight forwarders, and others required to register with the Department of Transportation, including information with respect to a carrier’s safety rating, compliance with required levels of financial responsibility, and compliance with the provisions of section 14504a. The Secretary shall ensure that Federal agencies, States, representatives of the motor carrier industry, and the public have access to the Unified Carrier Registration System, including the records and information contained in the System.

children.heading: .—Procedures for Correcting Information
children.label: c .—Procedures for Correcting Information
children.text: Not later than 60 days after the effective date of this section, the Secretary shall prescribe regulations establishing procedures that enable a motor carrier to correct erroneous information contained in any part of the Unified Carrier Registration System.

children.heading: .—Registration and filing evidence of financial responsibility
children.label: 1 .—Registration and filing evidence of financial responsibility
children.text: The fee for new registrants shall as nearly as possible cover the costs of processing the registration.

children.heading: .—Evidence of financial responsibility
children.label: 2 .—Evidence of financial responsibility
children.text: The fee for filing evidence of financial responsibility pursuant to this section shall not exceed $10 per filing. No fee shall be charged for a filing for purposes of designating an agent for service of process or the filing of other information relating to financial responsibility.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the fee system shall include a nominal fee for the access to or retrieval of information from the Unified Carrier Registration System to cover the costs of operating and upgrading the System, including the personnel costs incurred by the Department and the costs of administration of the unified carrier registration agreement.

children.label: i
children.text: to any agency of the Federal Government or a State government or any political subdivision of any such government for the access to or retrieval of information and data from the Unified Carrier Registration System for its own use; or

children.label: ii
children.text: to any representative of a motor carrier, motor private carrier, leasing company, broker, or freight forwarder (as each is defined in section 14504a) for the access to or retrieval of the individual information related to such entity from the Unified Carrier Registration System for the individual use of such entity.

children.heading: .—Use of Fees for Unified Carrier Registration System
children.label: e .—Use of Fees for Unified Carrier Registration System
children.text: Fees collected under this section may be credited to the Department of Transportation appropriations account for purposes for which such fees are collected and shall be available for expenditure for such purposes until expended.

children.heading: .—Application to Certain Intrastate Operations
children.label: f .—Application to Certain Intrastate Operations
children.text: Nothing in this section requires the registration of a motor carrier, a motor private carrier of property, or a transporter of waste or recyclable materials operating exclusively in intrastate transportation not otherwise required to register with the Secretary under another provision of this title.
